general_practice.description: The practice is licensed in terms of relevant legislation for the level and type of services provided.
The manager ensures that there is a system in place to monitor the implementation of applicable laws and regulations.
There is evidence of response to any reports from inspecting and regulatory authorities.
general_practice.guideline: CRITICAL   This requires documented evidence for whatever inspections that may have been conducted. Examples may include inspections by the following authorities The local fire authority for fire clearance purposes The national authority responsible for radiation safety aspects The national authority responsible for health and safety compliance The national authority responsible for financial compliance The national authority responsible for maintenance requirements such as boiler inspections and pressure tests, autoclave pressure tests The local authority responsible for testing of water supplies The relevant professional bodies where student training is provided Licensing authorities for facilities such as the pharmacy, etc. Immigration requirements for non-residence
general_practice.guidelines: CRITICAL   This requires documented evidence for whatever inspections that may have been conducted. Examples may include inspections by the following authorities The local fire authority for fire clearance purposes The national authority responsible for radiation safety aspects The national authority responsible for health and safety compliance The national authority responsible for financial compliance The national authority responsible for maintenance requirements such as boiler inspections and pressure tests, autoclave pressure tests The local authority responsible for testing of water supplies The relevant professional bodies where student training is provided Licensing authorities for facilities such as the pharmacy, etc. Immigration requirements for non-residence

general_practice.description: The manager implements processes to manage and control human, financial and other resources.
Information on services, hours of operation and processes for obtaining care is provided to services in the community who work in collaboration with the hospice and palliative care service.
There are documented processes for staffing the practice.
The desired education, qualifications, skills and knowledge are defined for personnel.
The processes include measures to improve personnel retention.
There is implementation of a reliable, documented process for evaluating and verifying the credentials of all health professionals
Personnel files contain copies of qualifications and licences/registration from the relevant authority for CRITICAL all health professionals.
There is a system to track the annual registration of all health professionals.
All personnel with direct contact with the public have had a police check, a copy of which is kept in their personnel file.
There are documented processes for orientation of personnel to the practice.
The practice supports continuing education for its clinical personnel and maintains records of this in personnel files.
There is a development strategy for the practice that ensures that the practice manager and administrative personnel receive the training required to fulfil their responsibilities.
Personnel members are informed of opportunities to participate in advanced education, training, research, and other experiences.
A designated person is responsible for the implementation and maintenance of a financial strategy
The designated person is suitably qualified and/or experienced in accounting and financial management.
The responsibilities of designated person include ensuring that policies and procedures for all financial functions are implemented.
There is a current budget for the practice.
A report is produced at least annually, setting out the financial position to date.
There is a mechanism for establishing the Reason for budget variation in either income or expenditure.
Capital investment proposals are subject to unanimous agreement among the partners or are agreed according to a voting system acceptable to all partners in the practice.
general_practice.guideline: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate agreement. This criterion will be scored “not applicable” in practices owned by a solo practitioner.
general_practice.guidelines: Documented evidence must be provided to demonstrate agreement. This criterion will be scored “not applicable” in practices owned by a solo practitioner.

general_practice.description: The practice has documented ethical and legal policies and procedures for the financial management of the practice that are implemented.
Internal and external financial audit systems which meet audit requirements are maintained.
Annual audited financial statements are produced within the required time frame.
There is a capital asset register, which is routinely maintained.
general_practice.guideline: Documented evidence will be required.
general_practice.guidelines: Documented evidence will be required.

general_practice.description: Assets are insured.
general_practice.guideline: This includes the practice assets and where applicable the physical structure.
general_practice.guidelines: This includes the practice assets and where applicable the physical structure.

general_practice.description: All health professionals provide evidence of professional indemnity insurance.
general_practice.guideline: Compliance will be measured during the personnel file audit.
general_practice.guidelines: Compliance will be measured during the personnel file audit.

general_practice.description: An individual is designated to control the ordering, storage, distribution and control of equipment and supplies used in the practice.
general_practice.guideline: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices’ equipment and supplies. Where a supply chain manager/stock controller is employed or the function is performed by someone other than the solo practice manager, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.
general_practice.guidelines: This criterion will automatically be scored compliant where a solo private practitioner manages the practices’ equipment and supplies. Where a supply chain manager/stock controller is employed or the function is performed by someone other than the solo practice manager, compliance will be assessed against the requirements set out in the position description.

general_practice.description: Policies and procedures relating to all aspects Of provisioning/supply chain management are Implemented.
Secure storage facilities are available.
Prescription pads, letterheads, investigation request forms, administrative records and other official documents are accessible only to authorised persons.
general_practice.guideline: Compliance will be verified by observation and personnel interviews.
general_practice.guidelines: Compliance will be verified by observation and personnel interviews.

general_practice.description: The practice reports on notifiable diseases to appropriate external public health agencies.
general_practice.guideline: CRITICAL Documented evidence is required.
general_practice.guidelines: CRITICAL Documented evidence is required.

general_practice.description: The practice has a waste management policy that is consistent with country-specific laws and regulations and includes the safe handling, storing and disposing of all different types of waste.
general_practice.guideline: The policy must include all relevant aspects of waste management, for example, identification, colour coding, handling, storage, disposal of etc. with special reference to clinical/healthcare waste, personal protective equipment (PPE), the management of spills and the reporting and investigation of waste related incidents.
general_practice.guidelines: The policy must include all relevant aspects of waste management, for example, identification, colour coding, handling, storage, disposal of etc. with special reference to clinical/healthcare waste, personal protective equipment (PPE), the management of spills and the reporting and investigation of waste related incidents.

general_practice.description: Documented certification is available from the relevant authority to show that the facility complies with applicable laws and regulations in relation to fire safety (fire clearance Certificate).
Annual personnel training in fire prevention and evacuation procedures is documented.
general_practice.guideline: A fire safety evacuation plan must be developed by the practice. Personnel training must be provided and evacuation exercises held annually. Documented evidence that the plan is physically rehearsed will be required.
general_practice.guidelines: A fire safety evacuation plan must be developed by the practice. Personnel training must be provided and evacuation exercises held annually. Documented evidence that the plan is physically rehearsed will be required.

general_practice.description: Documented evidence is available to show that the personnel participate in a rehearsal of theemergency plan at least annually.
Documented procedures which outline the processes to provide required information to individuals CRITICAL and agencies outside the practice when required by laws or regulations are implemented.
general_practice.guideline: Documented evidence of reporting is required. This includes notifiable disease reporting, other statistics required according to country-specific legislation and information provided to healthcare funders.
general_practice.guidelines: Documented evidence of reporting is required. This includes notifiable disease reporting, other statistics required according to country-specific legislation and information provided to healthcare funders.

general_practice.description: Personnel are trained in the implementation of quality management processes.
general_practice.guideline: Evidence of compliance could exist in the form of attendance registers at such training sessions, information sharing meetings, availability of training manuals, policies and procedures etc.
general_practice.guidelines: Evidence of compliance could exist in the form of attendance registers at such training sessions, information sharing meetings, availability of training manuals, policies and procedures etc.

general_practice.description: The supply of medical equipment is adequate to meet the needs of the practice
Policies and procedures for the storage, retrieval, retention, destruction and confidentiality and safety of employee/client records and personal information are implemented
general_practice.guideline: Policies and procedures must include at least a) – g) in the standard intent above.
general_practice.guidelines: Policies and procedures must include at least a) – g) in the standard intent above.

general_practice.description: The retention process provides the necessary confidentiality and security
general_practice.guideline: CRITICAL  This will be confirmed by observation.
general_practice.guidelines: CRITICAL  This will be confirmed by observation.

general_practice.description: All practice personnel are trained in basic resuscitation techniques at least every two years, with records of their attendance at such training.
general_practice.guideline: Documented evidence of nationally accepted training must be provided.
general_practice.guidelines: Documented evidence of nationally accepted training must be provided.

general_practice.description: Dispensed medication is securely and legibly labelled with relevant information as required by practice policy and country specific legislation.
A register is maintained of all medication dispensed.
general_practice.guideline: CRITICAL  Documented evidence is required.
general_practice.guidelines: CRITICAL  Documented evidence is required.

general_practice.description: Controlled medication is prescribed and dispensed in accordance with legislation and practice policy.
general_practice.guideline: CRITICAL   Compliance will be measured by examining the dispensing register, the controlled medication register and during the patient record audit.
general_practice.guidelines: CRITICAL   Compliance will be measured by examining the dispensing register, the controlled medication register and during the patient record audit.

general_practice.description: Prescriptions conform to all legal requirements.
general_practice.guideline: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.
general_practice.guidelines: Compliance will be verified during the patient record audit.

general_practice.description: Prescriptions conform to all legal requirements.
Controlled drugs are stored in a cabinet of substantial construction, for which only authorised personnel have the keys.
general_practice.guideline: Country-specific laws and regulations and facility policy will determine the nature of such medication.
general_practice.guidelines: Country-specific laws and regulations and facility policy will determine the nature of such medication.

general_practice.description: Controlled drugs are stored in suitable facilities which include but are not limited to: lockable storage facilities, ceiling cages, burglar guards and alarm systems with keypads.
general_practice.guideline: The purpose of this criterion is to ensure the safety of controlled medication during times when the practice is closed. Unlike 24-hour facilities, these unattended practices may be targeted and therefore additional security measures are required.
general_practice.guidelines: The purpose of this criterion is to ensure the safety of controlled medication during times when the practice is closed. Unlike 24-hour facilities, these unattended practices may be targeted and therefore additional security measures are required.

general_practice.description: Controlled drugs are accurately accounted for in a specific register which is updated contemporaneously and available for inspection.
Controlled drugs that have expired are disposed of in accordance with legal requirements.
general_practice.guideline: Compliance will be measured against medication control regulations to ensure that the information in the register is accurate and complies with the requirements.
general_practice.guidelines: Compliance will be measured against medication control regulations to ensure that the information in the register is accurate and complies with the requirements.

general_practice.description: Patient and family rights are identified in a policy and documented in accordance with relevant and current laws, charters and regulations.
general_practice.guideline: Patient and family rights are internationally accepted and implemented. Country specific patient rights charters must be referenced in the policy. The practice policy must include at least the right to: A healthy and safe environment Participation in decision-making Access to health care Ethical health care Choice of health services Confidentiality and privacy Culturally sensitive care Informed consent Refusal of treatment A second opinion Complaints about health services
general_practice.guidelines: Patient and family rights are internationally accepted and implemented. Country specific patient rights charters must be referenced in the policy. The practice policy must include at least the right to: A healthy and safe environment Participation in decision-making Access to health care Ethical health care Choice of health services Confidentiality and privacy Culturally sensitive care Informed consent Refusal of treatment A second opinion Complaints about health services

general_practice.description: The practice has a documented policy outlining the procedure for obtaining consent in the case of patients who are unable to grant consent for themselves by way of age or mental/physical incapacity, which is in accordance with the relevant laws and regulations.
